name: Baja California (Mexico)
description: A white field with the coat of arms of Baja California centred.
history: Baja California has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

features.role: coat of arms of Baja California
features.symbolism: The arms feature the sun rising over the peninsula, agricultural and industrial emblems, and a stylised Latin cross with a fish, all referencing the missions, fishing, agriculture, mining and the geographic position of the state on the Pacific peninsula.
